stories.title: Meloni presses von der Leyen for energy carveout in EU spending rules
stories.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is pressing Brussels to loosen the EU’s fiscal rules to help governments absorb the economic shock unleashed by the Iran war and soaring energy prices. In a letter to European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, first reported by Italy’s Corriere della Sera newspaper on Monday, Meloni requested that “investments and extraordinary measures needed to address the energy crisis” be exempted from the bloc’s Stability and Growth Pact

stories.title: Campaigners threaten legal action over Britain’s NHS deal with Donald Trump
stories.summary: LONDON — Health campaigners are threatening to bring the British government to court over its drug-pricing deal with Donald Trump, in a legal fight that risks undermining a key pillar of the U.S.-U.K. trade deal.  Patient advocacy group Just Treatment and social justice organization Global Justice Now have written to the new Health Secretary James Murray, accusing ministers of breaching parliamentary protocol as they rewrite NHS drug-pricing rules to meet Washington’s demands. Last month, a

stories.title: Xi Jinping prepares to welcome Vladimir Putin to China, four days after hosting Donald Trump
stories.summary: China’s deep relationship with Russia remains a continuing cause for concern in the west, particularly since Moscow launched its invasion of Ukraine Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin exchanged “congratulatory letters” on Sunday ahead of the Russian president’s visit to Beijing this week, four days since Donald Trump left China after a high-stakes summit . Xi, China’s leader, said bilateral cooperation between Russia and China had “continuously deepened and solidified”, with this year marking the 30t

stories.title: ‘An orgy of antisemitism is overtaking the west’: Son of Saul’s László Nemes on Hollywood hypocrisy
stories.summary: His extraordinary Auschwitz film won every award going. Now the Hungarian director is back with new drama Orphan, as well as a Jean Moulin biopic at Cannes. He talks about resurgent global prejudice – and refusing to be lectured by the film industry ‘overclass’ We’ve been talking for less than five minutes when I spot the swastika. It’s just above the head of László Nemes, one of Europe’s most acclaimed directors, as he sits in the suite of a London hotel, talking about Orphan, his intensely per

stories.title: Romaine calm: live frog found in discount supermarket lettuce bag
stories.summary: ‘Look, if I was in a French supermarket, I probably would have got a two for one deal on that one,’ woman in WA told national TV Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When a group of housemates in Western Australia bought a pair of lettuces in a sealed plastic bag – reduced for quick sale to $1.15 – they got two other things for which they could not have bargained. One was a live frog reckoned to be the size of a man’s

stories.title: Lammy dismisses Streeting’s call for UK to rejoin EU as ‘sixth form’ debating position – UK politics live
stories.summary: Deputy PM says the public will not forgive weeks of internal debate on the EU and Labour leadership, warning it will play into Reform’s hands A reader asks: Would Burnham be required to resign the post of mayor to fight a byelection. Or only in the event of winning one? The mayor of a strategic authority – as exists in Greater Manchester – may stand as a candidate in a parliamentary election and be elected as an MP. There is no legal requirement for the mayor of a strategic authority to resign i

stories.title: Kyiv and Budapest launch talks on the rights of Hungarian minorities in Ukraine
stories.summary: Kyiv and Budapest have agreed to hold talks regarding the rights of Hungarian minorities in Ukraine, Hungary’s foreign minister announced Monday in an upbeat sign for Ukraine’s EU membership bid. Anita Orbán, who was sworn in as Hungarian foreign minister earlier this month, wrote in a post on X that the discussions, which are set to kick off on Tuesday, would set the stage for the “prompt and reassuring settlement of minority rights issues.” The rights of Hungarian minor

stories.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
stories.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

stories.title: A sárgarépa a hollandok miatt lett narancssárga
stories.summary: Előtte lila, sárga és néha fehér volt, aztán a holland földművesek elterjesztették a narancssárga verziót. De a legendával ellentétben nem azért, hogy az uralkodóház előtt tisztelegjenek vele.

stories.title: A Bajnokok Ligája legnagyobb magyar sikereit nem a futballisták, hanem a bírók szállították
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. BL-sztorik, tizenkettedik rész.

stories.title: Tiborcz István török üzlettársától a Balkánon elköltött milliárdokig terjed a HEPA pénzszórása
stories.summary: Az állami HEPA papíron a magyar exportőrök megsegítésére jött létre, a szervezet működését viszont rendre átszőtték a politikailag jól bekötött üzleti körökhöz vezető szerződések és támogatások.

stories.title: Merkely Béla szerint Hankó Balázs kiváló gyógyszerész, csak nem ért a kultúrához
stories.summary: A Semmelweis Egyetem rektora szerint a modellváltás nagyrészt pozitív eredményeket hozott, de túlságosan átpolitizáltak a kuratóriumok.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás szerint nincs olyan jogi ok vagy alkotmányos indok, ami miatt le kéne mondania
stories.summary: Nem foglalkozik azzal, hogy Magyar Péter alkalmatlannak és méltatlannak tartja a köztársasági elnöki posztra, mert ez csak politikai vélemény.

stories.title: Napokon belül átrakhatják a Revolut-felhasználókat az új, magyar számláikra
stories.summary: A magyar számlákra közvetlenül is lehet majd már fizetést kérni.

stories.title: Orbán Anita: Megkezdődnek a szakértői szintű egyeztetések a kárpátaljai magyarság jogairól
stories.summary: Erről állapodott meg Andrij Szibiha ukrán külügyminiszterrel a hétvégén.

stories.title: Magyarországon építene üzemet a világ legnagyobb elektromosrobogó-gyártója
stories.summary: A Yadea Kínában már több mint százmillió robogót adott el, most Európába is betörne.

stories.title: Krausz Ferenc nyílt levelet írt Magyar Péternek, az alapítványuk teljes átvilágítását kérte
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas fizikus arról írt, hogy hátralévő élete és munkássága legnagyobb részét Magyarországnak szeretné szentelni.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Sulyok Tamásnak: Önnek távoznia kell! És távozni is fog
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök hosszan reagált az államfő hétfőn megjelent interjújára, azt írta, nincs miről egyeztetni.

stories.title: A Bethesda Gyermekkórház igazgatója: Nem leszek népszerű ezzel, de szülészetre sincsen szükség minden kórházban
stories.summary: Velkey György János elmondása szerint személyes kudarcként élte meg, hogy nem sikerült elérnie, hogy az Orbán-kormány prioritásként kezelje a gyermekegészségügyet.

stories.title: Élő környezetért felelős minisztérium szóvivője: A természetvédelem a lakosság megszólítása és bevonása nélkül nem lehet sikeres
stories.summary: Orbán Zoltán szerint nemcsak az állampolgárok, lakóközösségek, hanem az államigazgatási szervek gondolkodásmódját is át kell alakítani.

stories.title: Ukrán külügyminiszter: Készen állunk, hogy kölcsönösen előnyös fejezetet nyissunk az ukrán–magyar kapcsolatokban
stories.summary: Andrij Szibiha megköszönte Orbán Anitának a magyar kormány reakcióját Kárpátalját is érintő orosz rakétatámadásra.

stories.title: A tour inside the emblematic office buildings of the Orbán administration
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s iron bed, his chair engraved with a crown and a coat of arms, the palace-like interior of the Ministry of Interior, and the posh design of the Cabinet Office—we toured the former government’s buildings with Péter Magyar.

stories.title: Hoki-vb: peches vereség Ausztria ellen, a briteket kell verni a bentmaradásért
stories.summary: Második mérkőzését is elveszítette a magyar férfi jégkorong-válogatott a svájci világbajnokság zürichi csoportjában. Jól játszott a válogatott, minden erejét mozgósította az egyenlítésért, de végül 4–2-re kikapott az utolsó pillanatokban egy üres kapus találattal.

stories.title: Magyar Péter 15-20 zsák ledarált iratot talált Lázár János volt minisztériumának épületében
stories.summary: Feljelentést tesznek.

stories.title: Gulyás Magyarnak: A munkatársaim a tudtom nélkül törölték a posztomat, de nem ez a lényeg
stories.summary: A Havasi Bertalan felmentése körüli saga folytatódik.

stories.title: Drámai pillanatok után svéd győzelem a Tour de Hongrie-n, Fetter Erik lett a legjobb hegyimenő
stories.summary: Az összetettben vezető Söderqvist a veszprémi cél előtt nem sokkal bukott és elhúztak az ellenfelei, de végül befogta őket. Valter Attila legjobb magyarként 9. lett a bringás körversenyen.

stories.title: Az MNB-alapítvány lengyel cége olyan, mint az FC Barcelona: a focisták remekül keresnek, de a tulajdonos belerokkan
stories.summary: Az MNB-alapítvány legnagyobb befektetésének számító lengyel GTC körül egyre nagyobb a feszültség: a cég árfolyama zuhan, a részvényesek elégedetlenek, és miközben a vezetés a túlélésért és a vagyonmentésért küzd, a háttérben továbbra is ott lebegnek a Matolcsy-körhöz köthető szerződések.

stories.title: Leharcolt bíróságok képeivel reagált a Magyar Bírói Egyesület a Kúria épületére
stories.summary: A képeken leomlott vakolat, lomok, kikopott csempék láthatók, ezzel válaszoltak a Kúria fényűzésére.

stories.title: Azonosítottak egy nőt az Interpol különleges listájáról, és a program történetében először a gyilkost is elkapták
stories.summary: A „hordóban talált nőnek” összesen 21 évet kellett várnia ahhoz, hogy azonosítsák. Egy tragikus szerelmi háromszög vezethetett a meggyilkolásához, és valószínű, hogy nem ő az egyetlen áldozat. Hakima Boukerouisé az első olyan eset az Interpol programjában, ami vádemelésig jutott.

stories.title: Cseh László bekerült a hírességek csarnokába, Phelpsnek is megköszönte, hogy rávilágított a gyengeségeire
stories.summary: A kétszeres világbajnok úszónak motivációt jelentett, hogy zsenikkel versenyezhetett.

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: Roma children make history by performing Roma hymn at Hungarian parliament – video
stories.summary: Young, mostly Roma, members of the Sugo Tambura band realised their dream on Saturday by performing the Roma hymn at Hungary's parliament. The new prime minister, Péter Magyar, kept the promise he made to the children when he visited their village on the campaign trail. Roma rights campaigners have seized the moment, calling on the new government to ensure that the symbolism translates into real change Continue reading...

stories.title: Latvia: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigns over handling of drone incidents after coalition ally's withdrawal
stories.summary: Evika Silina says she is resigning her post, following the defense minister's resignation and the coalition government's collapse amid controversy over Ukrainian drones aimed at Russia that landed in the Baltic country.

stories.title: UAE blames Iran or proxies for strike near nuclear plant, as Trump tells Tehran ‘clock is ticking’
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi denounces ‘dangerous escalation’ as Iran war ceasefire grows more precarious, and US president voices impatience at stalemate The United Arab Emirates has blamed a fire near its nuclear power plant on a drone launched by Iran or one of its proxies in what the UAE called a “dangerous escalation”. The fire was just outside the Barakah nuclear plant and caused no injuries or radiation alerts, with the emirate’s nuclear regulator saying there was no radioactive leak or risk to the public.

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump warns ‘clock is ticking’ for Iran to reach peace deal
stories.summary: US president says there ‘won’t be anything left’ of country if it doesn’t come to an agreement UAE blames Iran or its proxies for drone strike fire near nuclear plant The October 2025 ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas explicitly required the immediate resumption of humanitarian aid. Over half a year into the ceasefire, the amount of aid being let into Gaza is still wholly inadequate to meet the needs of the population, despite a small increase from before the agreement. “Israel’s inte

stories.title: Moscow’s German front company: How the Russian military busted through sanctions
stories.summary: LÜBECK, Germany — The police and customs officers had been waiting for hours. Parked outside the Radisson Blu Senator Hotel in the northern German city of Lübeck, they sat in silence, engines off, watching the entrance.  Inside, Nikita S. — a 39-year-old businessman they suspected of running a smuggling operation supplying the Russian military industry with European technology — was enjoying what would be his last night of freedom. Shortly after 6 a.m., the officers moved in. Nikita S. was

stories.title: French star Patrick Bruel denies multiple sexual assault allegations
stories.summary: Singer and actor who has appeared in more than 40 films faces investigations in France and Belgium The French singer and actor Patrick Bruel, the subject of multiple rape allegations, protested his innocence on Sunday and said he would not step away from his work. The Paris prosecutor’s office announced earlier on Sunday that Bruel was the subject of at least four complaints of sexual assault in France, and that the cases would be investigated together. Agence France-Presse contributed to this r

stories.title: Trump’s immigration crackdown could cost up to $479bn in lost taxes over 10 years
stories.summary: Proposed IRS data sharing with immigration authorities and ICE raids have made filing risky for those who are undocumented The Trump administration’s immigration crackdown could cause the US to potentially lose up to $479bn in lost tax revenue over the the next 10 years, with enforcement deterring undocumented workers from filing their taxes this year, according to tax experts. Tax advisers say major changes, including proposed data sharing with immigration enforcement, have made filing taxes ri

stories.title: Introducing Stateside with Kai and Carter: Stacey Abrams on why gutting US Voting Rights Act is ‘evil’ – podcast
stories.summary: The US supreme court demolished the 1965 Voting Rights Act when it ruled in Louisiana v Callais in April that states can’t consider race in redistricting. Southern states from Tennessee to Alabama have rushed to erase majority Black districts, sparking chaos for the midterm elections. Kai Wright talks with Stacey Abrams , voting rights activist and former Georgia house minority leader, about the fallout from the decision and why she thinks the way forward is still through engaging more voters to

stories.title: Spain’s conservatives forced to rely on far-right Vox party after losing majority in Andalucía
stories.summary: People’s party wins regional election but loses absolute majority, opening door to possibly months of negotiations Spain’s conservative People’s party (PP) won Sunday’s Andalucían regional election , but lost its absolute majority, leaving it dependent on the support or abstention of the far-right Vox party to form a new government. After the poll in Spain’s most populous region – which will serve as a barometer of wider electoral opinion before next year’s general election – the socialists slum

stories.title: ‘A book that should be read by all Australians’: Clare Wright wins book of the year at the NSW Literary awards
stories.summary: The historian won $50,000 for her nonfiction book Näku Dhäruk: The Bark Petitions, which judges praised as deeply researched, ‘highly original’ and ‘vividly alive’ A “highly original” nonfiction by Melbourne historian Clare Wright, charting the creation of the Yirrkala Bark Petitions – a seminal moment in Australia’s history of land rights – has won book of the year at the NSW literary awards. The Petitions were landmark documents presented by Yolŋu elders to the Australian parliament in 1963 on

stories.title: Europe should pick negotiator for possible Russian talks, says Zelenskyy – Europe live
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says Europe must ‘have a strong voice and presence’ amid search for candidate who could lead any talks with Putin in Madrid In other news, Spain’s conservative People’s party (PP) won Sunday’s Andalucían regional election , but lost its absolute majority, leaving it dependent on the support or abstention of the far-right Vox party to form a new government. Continue reading...

stories.title: The French are hitting their protein goals - thanks to a cheese that looks like ectoplasm | Emma Beddington
stories.summary: La cancoillotte, a low-fat liquid cheese product, has somehow taken the country of haute cuisine by storm Exciting news from over the Channel: a viral cheese has dropped, but good luck spelling, or saying, it. As Libération reports , la cancoillotte (even native speakers struggle with the pronunciation, apparently), a liquid cheese from Franche-Comté in the east, is taking over fitness social media, thanks to its 16g of “ prot ” per 100g (as the French muscle bros and girls say), low fat co

stories.title: At least four people killed in Russia as Ukraine launches retaliatory strikes
stories.summary: Wave of almost 600 drones launched across 14 regions, after Moscow’s deadly three-day attack on Ukraine last week One of Ukraine’s largest ever drone strikes against Russia’s regions, including Moscow, has killed at least four people and wounded a dozen more, the Russian authorities have said. The wave of almost 600 Ukrainian drones struck overnight across 14 Russian regions, as well as the Crimean peninsula and the Black and Azov seas, the Russian defence ministry said on Sunday, with the area

stories.title: Cuba buys more than 300 drones from Russia and Iran — report
stories.summary: Cuba has also discussed plans to use drones to attack US targets such as the naval base at Guantanamo Bay, according to Axios news outlet.

stories.title: Thousands gather in Washington for America-themed prayer rally
stories.summary: Donald Trump and top Cabinet officials addressed the thousands of people gathered at the mass prayer event in Washington.

stories.title: Ukraine's Dnipro and Odesa targeted in Russian strikes
stories.summary: At least a dozen were injured in central and southern Ukraine overnight, local authorities said. The latest strikes come a day after Kyiv launched hundreds of drone strikes on Russian territory over the weekend.

stories.title: Germany news: Government plans civil defense expansion
stories.summary: Headlines in Germany today include Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt announcing plans to ramp up civil defense measures, and Chancellor Merz meeting euroskeptic Bulgarian Premier Rumen Radev. DW has more.

stories.title: Escaped tiger shot by German police after attacking man
stories.summary: The tiger escaped a facility owned by local trainer Carmen Zander, also known as Germany's "Tiger Queen", according to local media reports.

stories.title: Drone strike sparks fire near UAE nuclear plant amid Iran ceasefire tension
stories.summary: A drone strike sparked a fire near the UAE’s Barakah nuclear plant, officials say, with no injuries or radiation leak reported as regional tensions around the Iran ceasefire escalate.

stories.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
stories.summary: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

stories.title: Trump-backed challenger defeats Republican senator who voted to convict president
stories.summary: Trump threw his full support behind Julia Letlow in Louisiana's high-stakes contest, branding the incumbent "disloyal".

stories.title: Impose sanctions on Iran, Trump’s treasury chief tells finance ministers
stories.summary: PARIS — U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Monday urged allies to sanction Iran as peace talks between the two countries continue to stall. “We call on all the G7 and on all of our allies and the rest of the world to follow the sanctions regimes so that we can crack down on the illicit financing that is fuelling the Iranian war machine and get this money back to the Iranian people,” Bessent said as he entered a meeting of G7 finance ministers in Paris. Finance ministers and cen

stories.title: Idaho air force base locked down after midair collision, officials say
stories.summary: Four crew members are safe after incident involving aircraft during weekend air show at military base in western Idaho Four crew members are safe after two navy jets collided and crashed to the ground on Sunday at an air show at Mountain Home air force base in western Idaho , officials said. The collision involved two US navy EA-18G Growlers from the Electronic Attack Squadron 129 in Whidbey Island, Washington , said Cmdr Amelia Umayam, a spokesperson for Naval Air Forces, US Pacific Fleet. Cont

stories.title: ‘The end of the road’: the man on a mission to take Barcelona back from overtourism
stories.summary: José Antonio Donaire is not against tourists but wants to return the city to its residents – and he is starting with its most iconic market After decades of relentlessly marketing their vibrant Mediterranean city, the Barcelona authorities have appointed a man on a mission to say “no more” – and, he says, to return its most iconic market back to local residents. Last year, the Barcelona area attracted 26 million visitors, up 2.4% on 2024. The appointment of José Antonio Donaire as the city’s fir

stories.title: Spain has banned Francoist symbols. So why is the country still full of kitsch cafes glorifying the dictator? | Abbas Asaria
stories.summary: These unsettling and unusual places tell a vivid story about the unique way that Spain deals with its past – or fails to Una Grande Libre reads the sign above the entrance to a bar-restaurant in Madrid’s Usera neighbourhood. This was Francisco Franco’s motto for Spain – one, great, free – and it is accompanied by a large portrait of the dictator superimposed on to the window. The exteriors of El Cangrejo in Ciudad Real and Casa Pepe in Despeñaperros are a little bit more subtle, but not much: de

stories.title: National Conversation project launches as Britain risks ‘being torn apart by differences’
stories.summary: Commission led by Sajid Javid and Jon Cruddas asks public to share vision of community and country to help rebuild social cohesion Sajid Javid has warned the country risks “being torn apart by our differences” as figures from across public life launch a project that asks what it means to be British. The Independent Commission on Community and Cohesion, co-chaired by the former Conservative chancellor as well as the former Labour party policy chief Jon Cruddas , is urging the public to share thei

stories.title: Pianist Jayson Gillham says he never considered apologising for Gaza comments, trial hears
stories.summary: Federal court case begins for classical pianist who alleges Melbourne Symphony Orchestra unlawfully discriminated due to his views Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A classical pianist who alleges the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra unlawfully discriminated against him because of his views on Israeli forces killing Palestinian journalists says he did not consider apologising for making the statement at the centre of t

stories.title: Police running out of room to store illegal cigarettes they’ve seized amid Australia’s booming illicit trade
stories.summary: Inquiry into black market hears cost of destroying vapes has also soared, with some requiring manual dismantling Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police are struggling to store a growing wave of illegal cigarettes and vapes seized from criminals, with secure facilities at capacity and the high cost of destroying illicit products becoming prohibitive. The Australian federal police (AFP) has been spending as much as

stories.title: Starmer is not setting out timetable for his departure, says David Lammy
stories.summary: Deputy PM says Andy Burnham would be ‘great addition to parliament’ but leadership row is an ‘own goal’ for Labour Keir Starmer is not about to set a timetable for his departure from Downing Street, David Lammy, one of the prime minister’s closest cabinet allies, has said, urging Labour to get beyond the “spectacular own goal” of repeated leadership speculation. While allies of Starmer have suggested he could be willing to step aside if Andy Burnham wins next month’s Makerfield byelection and no

stories.title: Chalmers complains of ‘unhinged scare campaign’ against changes – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Data reveals how Coles and Woolworths switch promotions in sync You’re looking to buy an electric toothbrush. You’re in Woolworths and you see one of the Oral-B kits advertised for sale at $99.50. We understand that people will have strong views about the politics of this but it is the substance of the changes that we are making that matters most and the substance of what we are announcing today: tens of thousan

stories.title: Ryanair ‘confident’ it will avoid jet fuel shortage but warns of future fare rises
stories.summary: Airline says travellers are leaving it longer to book and those buying flights later this year could face higher prices Business live – latest updates Ryanair is “confident” it will not face a jet fuel shortage this summer amid fears over widespread cancellations linked to the Iran war, but warned that holidaymakers booking their flights later this year could face higher fares. Neil Sorahan, the chief financial officer at the budget airline, said he was “increasingly confident that we will not s

stories.title: Oil prices rise and bonds wobble as Iran war stokes inflation fears
stories.summary: Trump warning over peace talks drives up crude price as UK gilts hit by uncertainty over Starmer leadership Business live – latest updates Oil prices rose and global bonds wobbled on Monday, as fresh tensions in the Middle East fed inflation fears and bets that central banks will have to increase interest rates. Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil, rose on Monday, after an attack on a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Europe braces for swing from Arctic chill to extreme heat
stories.summary: Temperatures in France and England could rise by 15C, while hot air could to give way to snow in parts of US After a prolonged spell of cool conditions across much of Europe, a dramatic swing in temperatures is expected in the coming days as warmer air surges north into western and central parts of the continent. A large blocking high over the North Atlantic and slow-moving low pressure across southern Scandinavia dragged Arctic air southwards last week, sending temperatures 10-15C below the sea

stories.title: Executions worldwide hit highest level since 1981, Amnesty says
stories.summary: The human rights group Amnesty International confirmed that at least 2,707 people were put to death in 2025 but said the actual number was much higher. Despite these figures, there are indications of possible progress.

stories.title: Australia orders firms to sell stakes in rare earths miner
stories.summary: The treasurer ordered China-linked companies to divest of Northern Minerals to protect Australia's interests.

stories.title: Ebola in Africa: Why it's a constant threat
stories.summary: Ebola is highly contagious and deadly. It spreads internationally, such as in the May 2026 outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. But that doesn't automatically make it a pandemic.

stories.title: India news: Delhi rejects Dutch concerns on minority rights, press freedom
stories.summary: Questions over press freedom and right of minorities come from a "lack of understanding," of India's diversity and history, New Delhi officials have said. More on this story and more in our daily wrap of India news.

stories.title: MV Hondius docks in Rotterdam after hantavirus ordeal
stories.summary: The hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius cruise ship has arrived at its final destination, Rotterdam. The remaining crew on board, including two medics, will disembark the ship.

stories.title: Elversberg: Bundesliga newcomers from tiny town make history
stories.summary: A team from a town of just 7,000 inhabitants has made history by sealing promotion to become the 59th club to play in the Bundesliga.

stories.title: Tiger escapes enclosure near Leipzig, one person injured
stories.summary: An escaped tiger has injured a 73-year-old man near Leipzig, with police saying they shot the animal. German media reported the tiger was owned by a controversial performer.

stories.title: From escaping child marriage 'to an old pervert' to becoming Sierra Leone's first lady
stories.summary: The private world of one of Africa's most powerful political women is revealed in a BBC World Service exclusive.

stories.title: Inside the 'kill-zone' on Ukraine's front line, where new weapons have transformed war
stories.summary: Machines are increasingly replacing humans on the front line, but troops are still vital for defending territory.

stories.title: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
stories.summary: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.

stories.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air at US air show
stories.summary: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

stories.title: Taiwan will not provoke conflict nor give up sovereignty, says president
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te made his first direct response to Trump and Xi's meeting where they discussed Taiwan independence.

stories.title: Trump says 'there won't be anything left' of Iran if it does not quickly agree to a deal with the US
stories.summary: Trump says time is running out for Iran to enter into a nuclear deal with the United States to secure a permanent cessation to hostilities, as the risk of a renewed US-Israel war on Tehran amplifies in the region.

stories.title: Taiwan’s president defends US arms purchases that Trump called 'bargaining chip'
stories.summary: Trump’s calling of arms sales to Taiwan a “bargaining chip” has raised concerns on the self-governing island of a potential change in US foreign policy towards the territory after the US president’s latest visit to China.

stories.title: 'Everest Man' and 'Mountain Queen' break own records scaling world's tallest peak
stories.summary: Kami Rita Sherpa and Lakpa Sherpa summited Everest for the 32nd and 11th time, respectively.

stories.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
stories.summary: The agency added that the outbreak, with around 246 cases and 80 deaths, does not meet the criteria of pandemic emergency.

stories.title: North Korea's Kim calls on military to bolster frontline defences against 'arch enemy'
stories.summary: The plans come at a low point in relations with South Korea and Pyongyang unresponsive to Seoul's repeated offers for dialogue.

stories.title: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
stories.summary: The freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring dozens of others.

stories.title: Watch: Bulgaria welcomes home Eurovision winner Dara with trophy
stories.summary: Eurovision winner Dara returned to Sofia to cheering fans after securing Bulgaria's first-ever victory at the song contest in Vienna.

stories.title: Japan arrests US nationals after one entered enclosure at viral baby monkey Punch's zoo
stories.summary: The baby monkey became an internet star this year after the zoo posted photos of Punch clutching an IKEA plush orangutan for comfort after being rejected by his mother.

stories.title: Bodybuilders show off muscles at Southeast Asian championships
stories.summary: Myanmar hosts the Southeast Asian Bodybuilding Championships in Yangon, with athletes from eight countries competing from May 14 to 18.

stories.title: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace progress stalls
stories.summary: On Monday Iran said it had responded to the latest US proposal and that exchanges with Washington were continuing.

stories.title: Israeli forces boarding Gaza-bound flotilla near Cyprus, activists say
stories.summary: Live broadcasts from the Global Sumud flotilla show commandos boarding several boats trying to breach the Gaza maritime blockade.

stories.title: UAE reports strike near Abu Dhabi nuclear power plant
stories.summary: The country is investigating the source of the strike, which it calls a "dangerous escalation".

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon hours after extension of ceasefire
stories.summary: Three paramedics at health centre among dead, while Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad killed in Gaza strike Israel carried out airstrikes in southern Lebanon, killing at least six people, including three paramedics working at a health centre, just hours after its envoys had agreed with the Lebanese government to extend a ceasefire . Israel also said it had killed the Hamas military chief, Izz al-Din al-Haddad, in a targeted strike in Gaza on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Top of Labour in row over EU as Nandy challenges Streeting’s remarks
stories.summary: Ex-health secretary’s allies rally to defend him after culture secretary calls his comments about rejoining bloc ‘odd’ A row has broken out at the top of the Labour party over whether Britain should try to rejoin the EU after Wes Streeting said the country should eventually seek to regain membership. Streeting, who resigned as health secretary on Thursday in protest at Keir Starmer’s leadership, kicked off a war of words after he argued on Saturday that Britain’s future lay back in the EU. Conti

stories.title: Minnesota deploys national guard to help fight wildfires in northern region
stories.summary: Tim Walz, the state’s governor, calls blazes ‘unpredictable and fast-moving’ as dry, windy weather fuels them Minnesota ’s national guard has been activated to help battle wildfires burning in the northern part of the state after the department of natural resources requested additional support. Governor Tim Walz authorized the deployment by issuing an executive order that declared a peacetime emergency. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russia in massive overnight attack
stories.summary: Ukraine has launched almost 600 drones at Russia in one of its largest overnight attacks amid Moscow's invasion. The region around the Russian capital was one of those targeted.

stories.title: German singer Heino pushes back against the far-right AfD
stories.summary: A far‑right politician in Germany used schlager icon Heino in election advertising without his consent. Heino is now demanding compensation for pain and suffering.

stories.title: New Epstein victims turn up in French probe — prosecutor
stories.summary: France is pursuing its own investigation into the Epstein affair, looking at potential crimes committed in the EU country or involving French nationals tied to the US sex offender.

stories.title: US: Two jets collide midair at Idaho air show
stories.summary: Two EA-18G Growler jets crashed near a US Air Force base in Idaho. The pilots ejected safely, organizers said.

stories.title: Middle East: Trump warns Iran 'time is ticking' on deal
stories.summary: The US president said the Iranians should agree to a deal "or there won't be anything left of them" amid stalled peace talks. DW has more.

stories.title: Canadian from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive
stories.summary: The individual is one of four former passengers on the MV Hondius isolating on Vancouver Island, British Columbia.

stories.title: Man drives car into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight
stories.summary: Eight people were injured, four seriously, before passers-by gave chase and stopped the man.

stories.title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
stories.summary: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.

stories.title: Eurovision winner Dara arrives to screaming fans in Bulgaria
stories.summary: The 27-year-old's tune Bangaranga won Bulgaria its first ever title in the song contest.

stories.title: World Urban Forum opens in Baku with warning on global housing divide
stories.summary: Ministers and experts opened the World Urban Forum in Baku on Sunday with stark warnings about the global housing divide, calling for international cooperation to tackle a growing crisis.

stories.title: Évtizedekig fizetjük majd azt, amit most a védett benzinárral spórolunk
stories.summary: Az üzemanyagárhoz politikai okokból nem lehet hozzányúlni, ezért a mostani árengedmény költségeit szétporlasztják a következő évekre, pufferként használva az ország stratégiai tartalékát.

stories.title: Úgy átadta a Fidesz március végi ünnepléssel a szokolyai piacot, hogy az azóta se nyitott ki
stories.summary: Március végén Vitályos Eszter és Rétvári Bence is elment egy szokolyai piacátadóra, amiről Szokolya önkormányzata még a választások előtt pár nappal is posztolt. A piaci átadóra őstermelőket és helyieket hívtak, de azóta is ez az egyetlen nap, amikor nyitva volt a piac.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt a hantavírusról: Ez nem Covid 2.0
stories.summary: Az egészségügyi miniszter szerint nincs ok a pánikra, mert a hantavírus másképp terjed, mint a Covid.

stories.summary: Az orosz védelmi minisztérium szerint az ukránok közel 600 drónnal támadtak, célba vették Moszkvát is.

stories.title: Török Gábor szerint Sulyok Tamás továbbra sem érti

stories.title: Hosszabb nyitvatartással készül a NAV az adóbevallás beadásának határidejére
stories.summary: Akár munkaidőn kívül is hívható lesz a NAV ügyfélszolgálata.

stories.summary: Keressük a kampányidőszak legbizarrabb történetét. Melyik borított ki jobban minket: amit Hann Endrével művelt a propaganda, vagy a tiszás belső ember?

stories.summary: Annyi pénzt szórt ki az előző kormány, hogy három hónap alatt jelentőset ugrott az államadósság.

stories.summary: Az új országgyűlési képviselők zömének van diplomája, és nagy többségük ért valamilyen idegen nyelven is. A parlament honlapjáról gyűjtöttük össze, ki mennyi papírt szerzett életében.

stories.summary: Legutóbbi beszámolói szerint a két érintett cég pénztárában és számláin együttesen több mint 15 milliárd forint pihent, de hiába, ha a zárolt számlákról a NAV-nak sem lehet utalni.

stories.title: 150 millió forintért, a kikiáltási ár duplájáért kelt el Vaszary János képe
stories.summary: Egy Zsolnay váza pedig világrekordhoz közeli összegért, 26 millió forintért talált új tulajdonosra a Virág Judit Galéria tavaszi árverésén.

stories.summary: De ha kényelmesebb, a jégmellény is működhet.

stories.summary: A rendőrségi nyomozás végül arra jutott, a pap tette ugyan „erkölcsileg kifogásolható, de nem bűncselekmény”.

stories.summary: A lapnak nyilatkozó egyik építési vállalkozó szerint még legalább hat hónap kellene, hogy befejezzék az 1500 négyzetméteres ház építését.

stories.summary: Közleményükben azt írták, a hatósági érvelés szerint megszűnt a kiutasítás jogalapja.

stories.summary: Szerinte ma már sokan viszonylagos biztonságban megszólalhatnak arról, hogy mi történt velük, de általánosságban ez nem igaz minden nőre.

stories.summary: Mivel őt nevezték ki a Tisza-kormány közlekedési és beruházási miniszterének. A Podmaniczky Mozgalom frakcióját is más vezeti majd.

stories.summary: Army supported by Russian mercenaries launches airstrikes after offensive by coalition of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists Mali’s armed forces, supported by Russian mercenaries, have launched airstrikes targeting a rebel alliance of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists as the ruling junta struggles to maintain its hold on power in the unstable west African country. stories.summary: BBC Verify has examined dozens of videos of fibre-optic drone attacks carried out by the Lebanese armed group.

stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. stories.title: Arrest of Iraqi terror suspect with alleged links to Iran’s Quds Force is astonishing but not surprising
stories.summary: Iran has a long history of unconventional operations, all designed to divert, distract and destabilise current or potential enemies The arrest by US authorities of an alleged Iraqi commander of an Iranian-backed militia group now accused of responsibility for 18 terrorist attacks in the UK, Europe and Canada since the beginning of the Iran war is an astonishing development – yet not the least bit surprising. According to a complaint unsealed on Friday in a federal court in Manhattan, Mohammad Ba

stories.title: Canada confirms first hantavirus case in isolation in British Columbia
stories.summary: The person was onboard the MV Hondius, the center of the outbreak that has claimed three lives Canadian officials said on Saturday that a test for one of the four Canadians currently quarantining in British Columbia after being exposed to the hantavirus while on board the cruise ship where the outbreak occurred indicated a positive result. Speaking at a news conference , Dr Bonnie Henry, British Columbia’s provincial health officer, said the individual developed mild symptoms, including fever an

stories.title: ‘Feels like an illusion’: inside post-Maduro Venezuela’s bewildering new era
stories.summary: Four months after Trump’s surprise raid, a political thaw has descended – but mingled with hope is trepidation for what comes next When Ángel Linares heard a strange buzz followed by an explosion, his first thought was that neighbours were setting off fireworks to celebrate the new year. Then his windows shattered, the building’s walls shook and its facade was ripped off, sending him flying on to the ground of an apartment suddenly reduced to rubble. His 85-year-old mother, Jesucita, feared Vene

stories.title: Az idei év egyik legjobb tinifilmje egy videójáték
stories.summary: A Mixtape eddig az idei év egyik legizgalmasabb játéka, nagyon jó a kreatív történetmesélésben, miközben több elemében egy interaktív animációs filmre hajaz.

stories.summary: Van, amit két nap alatt már meg is valósított a Tisza-kormány, van, amihez évek kellenek, és olyan is, amire akarat lenne, csak egyelőre pénz nem biztos, hogy lesz rá. stories.summary: Még úgy sem, hogy 31-30-ra most legyőzték Szerbiát.

stories.title: 'From collection to connection': How museums are redefining themselves
stories.summary: Gone are the days when museums were just places to see art and artifacts. On International Museum Day, DW looks at how museums are becoming unique spaces of community engagement and participation.

stories.title: Study: Immigrants disadvantaged in German housing market
stories.summary: The German housing market crisis is deepening social inequality. Newcomers in particular are struggling to find affordable housing — with consequences for integration, education, and the labor market.

stories.title: Germany stakes claim in new space race
stories.summary: German companies of all sizes and all over the country are developing satellites and space applications. They want to be part of the lucrative industry, which is increasingly crucial for civilian and military purposes.

stories.title: Germany news: UNICEF says child well-being ranking 'alarming'
stories.summary: A new study ranks Germany near the bottom in child well-being and education. Meanwhile, an escaped tiger was killed by police after injuring a man near Leipzig. DW has more.

stories.title: Dead humpback whale off Denmark is 'Timmy'
stories.summary: Divers have confirmed that a dead humpback whale spotted off Denmark is the same creature that spent weeks beached off Germany's Baltic coast. It comes two weeks after a contentious rescue operation.

stories.title: Two Nepali climbers break own Everest records
stories.summary: Two veteran Nepali climbers, a man and a woman, have each set new records for ascents of the world's highest mountain. The 492 Everest climbing permits issued by Nepal for the 2026 spring season represent another high.

stories.title: Putin to visit China days after Trump's Beijing trip, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping will discuss ways to deepen their countries' "comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation," the Kremlin said.

stories.title: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
stories.summary: Staff Sgt Mohamed Mahdhee died while diving in the search-and-rescue operation, the Maldives government tells the BBC.

stories.title: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
stories.summary: No group has yet claimed responsibility for the attacks, which have devastated Mussa town in Borno state.

stories.title: London on edge as Tommy Robinson backers and pro-Palestinian marchers rally
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people joined rival London marches for Tommy Robinson and Palestine, as 4,000 officers used strict routes to avoid clashes and keep order.

stories.title: Taiwan insists it is independent after Trump warning
stories.summary: Fresh from his summit in Beijing, the US president warned against a formal declaration of independence by the island.

stories.title: South Korea: Thousands watch robot monks march through Seoul’s historic streets
stories.summary: Four robot monks in traditional robes joined Seoul’s lantern parade ahead of Buddha’s birthday, symbolising Buddhism’s tech-savvy modernisation.

stories.title: Eurovision final in Vienna overshadowed by protests over Israel’s participation
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Vienna against Israel’s participation in Eurovision 2026 as security was tightened around the Wiener Stadthalle before the grand final, amid tensions linked to the war in Gaza.

stories.title: Foreign tourists are falling out of love with Goa - here's why
stories.summary: Foreign visitors to Goa have nearly halved from their pre-Covid peak even as domestic tourism is booming.

stories.title: The girl group who've sold out venues and toured the world - without releasing a record
stories.summary: The all-female supergroup has sold out venues across the UK and Ireland, toured stadiums with Ed Sheeran, and built a global following - all without releasing a debut record.

stories.title: Secret police and propaganda: The Chinese agents spying on expats in US
stories.summary: Experts say two espionage cases in the US highlight China's attempts to peddle influence globally over the past decade.

stories.title: Lights, camera, Bangaranga: Highlights from Eurovision
stories.summary: Bulgarian singer Dara stormed to victory in the contest, while Ukraine broke the record for the longest note ever sung at Eurovision.

stories.title: 'Look Mum, one point': Why does the UK keep getting Eurovision wrong?
stories.summary: After four years of flops, the BBC has to think hard about how it approaches Eurovision in 2027.

stories.title: Ebola outbreak is a global health emergency, WHO says
stories.summary: The World Health Organization said that it could be a "much larger" outbreak than what is currently being identified and reported but that is does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency.

stories.title: Threatened indictment of Raúl Castro ratchets up US pressure on Cuba
stories.summary: Trump administration move echoes indictment of Venezuela’s Nicolás Maduro as fuel crisis racks Cuba Tensions between Cuba and US seem set to rise further amid reports that Raúl Castro, the country’s 94-year-old former president, may soon face the type of indictment that led to the US abduction of the Venezuelan leader, Nicolás Maduro, in January. Although Raúl is officially retired, he remains the most potent figure in Cuban politics following the death of his brother Fidel in 2016, and by targe

stories.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo
stories.summary: Uganda also reports outbreak and health officials say cases were caused by Bundibugyo strain of virus An outbreak of Ebola has killed 65 people in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, health officials said. There have been 246 suspected cases of the haemorrhagic fever reported so far in the conflict-hit Ituri province, which shares borders with Uganda and South Sudan. Continue reading...

Family and supporters of the 78-year-old British citizen had hoped the US president could help secure his release. Continue reading...

stories.title: Funeral in Jabalia after Israeli strike hits Gaza wake despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Residents of Jabalia bury two Palestinians killed in an Israeli strike on a wake, as Gaza reports nearly 73,000 dead and truce violations persist.

stories.title: Israel confirms Hamas armed wing chief killed in Gaza strike
stories.summary: The military said Haddad was “among the last senior commanders in Hamas’s military wing responsible for planning and carrying out the October 7th massacre.”

stories.title: ‘Extremely cruel and tragic’: Iranian director Asghar Farhadi speaks out against state violence and the war
stories.summary: The film-maker, who won the Grand Prix for A Hero in 2021, condemned both the killing of protesters and the conflict’s bombing campaigns during a Cannes press conference Oscar-winning Iranian director Asghar Farhadi has described the deaths of civilians in Iran as “extremely cruel and tragic” during a press conference at the Cannes film festival. Farhadi, whose new Paris-set drama Parallel Tales premiered on the Croisette on Thursday night, was asked about working free from censorship in France,

stories.title: Shaky truce between Israel and Lebanon extended for 45 days, US says
stories.summary: Statement following ‘productive’ talks in Washington comes as Israel launches strikes on southern city of Tyre Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 45-day extension of their ceasefire after another round of talks in Washington, the US state department has said. It came after two “productive” days of talks, and more negotiations would be held from 2-3 June, the department spokesperson Tommy Pigott said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Asteroid 2026 JH2 — no need to worry about it hitting Earth
stories.summary: This big asteroid was discovered shortly before it was expected to hurtle past Earth. Any time you see a headline about asteroids approaching the planet, you can relax. Big and small asteroids fly by all the time.

stories.title: UK politics: How Starmer survives as Farage waits in wings
stories.summary: High profile resignations, a prime minister on the edge and populists on the up — UK politics is far from dull. DW explains what's going on in Keir Starmer's Labour party and with Nigel Farage. And what may be next.

stories.title: Ukraine: Dozens of European countries sign up to special tribunal plan to prosecute Russia
stories.summary: At talks in Moldova, more than 30 countries have said they will join a future special tribunal for Ukraine prosecuting Russia over its invasion. How the body will force Russians to stand trial is not clear.

stories.title: Islamic feminism more widespread in Bosnia and Herzegovina
stories.summary: Many Bosnian Muslim women want to attend mosque on Fridays and to play a greater role in decision-making. Slowly the official structures of the Islamic Community are changing.

stories.title: Germany news: Chancellor Merz says he wouldn't advise his kids to go to the US
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he wouldn't currently advise young Germans like his children to move to the US to study or work. He cited "the social climate that has suddenly developed."

stories.title: Iraq swears in new prime minister as US presses Baghdad to disarm Iran-backed groups
stories.summary: Iraqi parliament approved Ali al-Zaidi's government on Thursday, with the new prime minister vowing a state monopoly on weapons as Washington leans on Baghdad to disarm Iran-backed groups.

stories.title: Underwhelming summit outcome in China brings Trump back to reality
stories.summary: After raising high expectations ahead of his trip to Beijing, the U.S president leaves with little to show for it, disappointing investors. On key flashpoints such as Iran and Taiwan, China didn't give any ground.

stories.title: Watch: Ukraine and Russia swap 205 prisoners each in major exchange deal

stories.title: Women's rights and demographics take centre stage at regional forum in Bukhara
stories.summary: Regional leaders and UN officials gathered in Bukhara to discuss how investing in women, education and demographic planning could shape long-term development across Asia.

stories.title: Watch: What did we learn from Trump's visit to China?
stories.summary: President Trump has concluded a two-day visit to Beijing at a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

stories.title: Five Italians die in Maldives cave diving tragedy
stories.summary: Rescue teams in the Maldives continued recovery operations after the country’s deadliest diving accident killed five Italian divers.

stories.title: What is the 'Thucydides Trap' that Xi invoked in his meeting with Trump?
stories.summary: Xi Jinping opened his Beijing summit with Trump by asking whether the two powers could avoid the "Thucydides Trap" — then warned that mishandling Taiwan could lead to war.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon extend their ceasefire by 45 days following talks in the US
stories.summary: The US State Department will hold negotiations aimed at reaching a permanent political agreement on the 2 and 3 June. Beforehand, the Pentagon is to bring together military delegations from the countries concerned on 29 May.

stories.title: A call to stop the global housing crisis: World Urban Forum, why it matters?
stories.summary: More than 40,000 participants are set to gather in Baku for the UN's World Urban Forum as UN-Habitat warns that 3 billion people lack adequate housing and slum populations could triple by 2050.

stories.title: Trump says 'most active terrorist in the world' killed in US-Nigerian operation
stories.summary: The "meticulously planned and very complex mission" eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the Islamic State group's second in command globally, Trump said.

stories.title: Trump's independence warning prompts response from Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's foreign ministry said in a statement following Trump's comments that "Beijing has no right to claim jurisdiction over Taiwan."

stories.title: UN pleads for Equatorial Guinea not to send US asylum seekers to their home countries: ‘Their life would be in danger’
stories.summary: Human rights experts make rare public appeal as US deportees describe being held in ‘prison-like’ conditions Human rights experts at the United Nations issued a rare public appeal to Equatorial Guinea, urging the central African country to halt its plans to return US deportees to their home countries, where they face political violence, torture and death. The statement , co-signed by a representative of the African Commission on Human and People’s Rights, adds diplomatic pressure on Equatorial G

stories.title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
stories.summary: Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports. In an undisclosed April meeting at the justice department, Trump’s personal lawyer, Robert J Giuffra Jr, said that Adani would invest $10bn in the US economy a

stories.title: German intelligence offices snub US-based Palantir software
stories.summary: Germany's domestic intelligence agency has reportedly chosen a data analysis system from France, instead of US-based Palantir. Civil society has welcomed the move, but says more must be done.

stories.title: North Korea slams UK over sanctions on camp allegedly linked to abducted Ukrainian children
stories.summary: Since the war in Ukraine erupted in 2022, 2,318 Ukrainian children remain missing, while 20,570 have been deported or forcibly displaced and 704 killed, according to a Ukrainian government portal.

stories.title: EU preparing to offer key concession to UK in new post-Brexit agricultural deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Britain expected to be allowed to keep ban on live animal exports, sources say, in fillip for Keir Starmer UK politics live – latest updates Brussels is preparing to offer Keir Starmer a key concession in talks over an agricultural deal, giving the beleaguered prime minister an important victory in his efforts to move closer to the EU. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: Judge orders Trump administration to return Colombian woman deported to DRC back to the US
stories.summary: Judge called Adriana Maria Quiroz Zapata’s deportation to the Democratic Republic of Congo ‘likely illegal’ A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to bring a Colombian woman back to the US from the Democratic Republic of Congo , after she was deported to the African country that had refused to accept her. The deportation of Adriana María Quiroz Zapata “was likely illegal”, the US district judge Richard Leon ruled on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's shortfall in medium-range defense capabilities
stories.summary: The US has announced that it will not be deploying Tomahawk cruise missiles in Germany. Now the German government is looking for alternatives. Could drones be part of the solution?

stories.title: Will Ukraine corruption scandal reach Volodymyr Zelenskyy?
stories.summary: A drawn-out corruption affair in Ukraine has widened, with authorities now taking action against Andriy Yermak, former head of the presidential office. Will the scandal reach all the way to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy?

stories.title: Foreign student fee hikes in France trigger backlash
stories.summary: A French government plan to charge students from outside the European Union 16 times more than previously has stoked discontent. But how does this compare to the rest of Europe, both inside the EU and out?

stories.title: US reportedly seeks to indict Cuba's ex-president Raúl Castro as energy crisis deepens
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has renewed an offer of €85 million in aid on the condition that the assistance be distributed by the Catholic Church, bypassing the government.

stories.title: Protests erupt in Havana as Cuba faces severe fuel shortages
stories.summary: Small protests broke out in Havana over worsening shortages of food, power, and fuel, as Cuban authorities blamed US sanctions for the crisis and warned that fuel reserves had run out.

stories.title: Death toll from large-scale Russian attack that slammed Ukraine rises to 24
stories.summary: The drone and missile barrage, that mostly targeted Kyiv, marked one of Russia's largest attacks in the past four years. Many EU leaders voiced support for Ukraine.

stories.title: Delta Goodrem Eclipse Eurovision second semi-final performance in full — video
stories.summary: Australia’s 2026 Eurovision hopeful Delta Goodrem delivers a powerful performance on the 70th anniversary of the global song contest, becoming the first Australian act to qualify for the grand final since 2023. Heading into the competition as an early favourite behind Eurovision heavy hitters Denmark and Finland, Goodrem delivers a note-perfect rendition of her power-ballad entry, Eclipse. Her performance marks the 11th time Australia has appeared in the multinational song contest Eurovision 202

stories.title: Weather tracker: Furnace Creek sizzles as snow sweeps Siberia in a week of extremes
stories.summary: Record heat in North and Central America coincides with egg-sized hailstones in eastern China Extreme weather across several parts of the world this week has brought record-breaking temperatures to Honduras, North America and Indonesia. Honduras smashed its all-time May maximum temperature record earlier this month – only for it to be broken again on 13 May in Choluteca, known as the furnace of Central America. Temperatures climbed to 42.2C (107.9F), surpassing the previous record of 42.1C. With

stories.title: Why men are less worried than women about climate change
stories.summary: From petro-masculinity to electric trucks: why men disengage from climate action and what might bring them back.

stories.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
stories.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

stories.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
stories.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

stories.title: Ceasefire in sight? What's next for Russia's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: Four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, is the end finally in sight? DW spoke to US political experts about why they think US home elections could bring about a ceasefire.

stories.title: US drops bond requirement for FIFA World Cup ticket holders
stories.summary: Fans from five African World Cup qualifying nations with valid tickets are exempt from paying a hefty bond. But fans from several other countries still face major hurdles to seeing their teams play in the United States.

stories.title: China goes electric, but can it get off coal?
stories.summary: The world's clean energy superpower also emits the most CO2. But coal-fired power may have peaked as China electrifies its economy with ever more renewables.

stories.title: Honda reports first loss since 1957 as it waters down EV strategy, but shares rise on 2026 forecasts
stories.summary: The Japanese carmaker pursued an aggressive switch towards electric vehicles that it's now trying to walk back. Despite posting its first loss since 1957, its share price spiked on more buoyant predictions for 2026.
